item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just listened to the SCP Archives rendition of this, and came by to say how much I loved it. Especially the jovial attitude of the help desk, even when it knows it’s about to screw the foundation.

Quite fun +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of my favorite articles on the site, but…

Why don't they just ask it how to suppress the information it gives them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Helpful desk receptionist able to tell you about anything.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this skip! SCP-3120-1 is adorable, too! +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I'm left wondering if there's any connection between this phone and SCP-2315, since they both are phones and broker in information. But the meaning and execution of each is completely different.

(So, incidentally, for new writers, this is how you can have an idea that has been "done before" but still turn it into something completely unique. Add detail.)

This one is fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This could easily both save the world and destroy it, possibly in the same action. And to think its an Iphone…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is, especially from an in- universe perspective, one of the most ominous SCPs I've read— and even that is simply because of the reason it was created.

Most people would cite the greatest fear of mankind, I think, as the fear of the unknown. This to me seems to propose another idea— the greatest fear held by humans is a secret revealed. It seems to me to harken back to social conformity- very few people like being stood mentally naked and judged for what they are.

Masterful work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. I love this. Putting the Foundation in such a dilemma every time an object breaches containment is genius, especially by the same tool they rely on. You have to wonder what the success rate of their anti-memes are; there's bound to be at least one person who remembers what happened at Älveå, and they're not pleased at all. Hell, if the world after that isn't in a Broken Masquerade situation, I'd at least imagine there's some juicy material to write from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely beautiful. I want to point out flaws but I can't find any. Be back once I reread to find something to nitpick. Upvoted!

Edit: Found it. I think "destroyed" is too general a term when referring to the Swedish capital. It would be extra horrifying/clinical (in keeping with the Foundation being cold) to specify the means of destruction. The city being firebombed out of existence, for example, hits a lot harder than merely "destroying" it. Firebombing is just an example, I'm sure there are better options.

In fact, I know this would be a lot of work and may not fit in to the whole plotline, but reading the transcript of the actual conversation about the city would be cool. Something about this help desk lady cheerfully advising someone to destroy an entire city is creepy in an awesome way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't worry, you didn't make me uncomfortable! I'm glad you enjoyed my work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No need to apologise, I'll just tun with more complex coding

3114

3-1-14
which is the HEX for
3-1-20
which is the letter-number for
C-A-T

and I just… love cats (o_o )

and I checked out your other SCPs, which I also enjoyed, some had seen, but never realised were from the same author

Again, there was no need for apologising, I didn't mean to discomfort you if I have done it in some way, sorry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This…it's perfect. The story elements, the SCP itself, the drawbacks, the alluding to another reality to create hesitation in the use of the object.

Fantastic work, Canned, heartily upvoting this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
3120? I guess because I felt like it fit well, I'm sorry if you wanted to use it, but I guess that's just how it goes.

And I'll probably be making an author page soon; in the mean time, here are my other pages, in the order I made them:

+ other pages
- other pages

SCP-3222
SCP-3027
SCP-3055
SCP-3037
SCP-3096
SCP-3080

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the SCP itself is interesting, but…

WHY THE NUMBER, IT WAS MY FAVOURITE TnT

otherwise, I enjoyed it, where can I find more from you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done. I'm glad to see this go up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My seventh SCP! Thanks to Bentu and Modern_Erasmus for feedback.

+ Image attribution
- Image attribution

The image at the end (the 'anti-memetic agent') is mine; I made it out of three other images that are also mine.

The image of a Galaxy S7 Edge at the top of the page is from Wikimedia Commons.

Source: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/7/7e/Samsung_Galaxy_S7_Edge.png

Creator: Rodrigo.Argenton
